claim: Context engineering with PRP (Product Requirements Prompt) framework enables AI coding assistants to one-shot production-ready agent code by providing hypertuned architectural context upfront
actionable: Before prompting AI assistant for agent code, write detailed initial.md covering dependencies, tools, agent workflows, examples, and gotchas; generate full PRP using AI; then execute
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: PRP framework is 3-step: (1) write initial.md describing agent requirements, (2) use AI to expand into full PRP with examples/best practices/patterns, (3) execute PRP to generate code
actionable: Follow the 3-step PRP workflow: describe requirements → generate PRP with AI → execute PRP; don't skip generation step; validate each PRP before execution
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: Hypertuned PRPs for specific frameworks (Pydantic AI, MCP servers) reduce hallucination by pre-loading best practices, patterns, and documented examples for that tech stack
actionable: Reuse framework-specific PRP templates (Pydantic AI, FastAPI, etc.); build institutional PRP templates capturing your team's patterns to reduce iteration on new projects
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: AI agents require three core components: (1) dependencies (APIs, DB connections), (2) agent definition (system prompt, model), (3) tools (functions/MCP servers)
actionable: When defining agent requirements in initial.md, structure around these 3 pillars; be specific about each; provide examples for all three to enable assistant's code generation
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: Validation gates in PRP (unit tests, integration tests, security checks) enforce code quality; ask AI to iterate until tests pass before accepting generated code
actionable: In PRP, define validation gates including test suites, error handling checks, and security validation; ask AI to verify code against gates before finalizing
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: Include 'gotchas' section in initial.md listing common mistakes the AI assistant makes (env vars, authentication flows, API pagination); this directly improves output quality
actionable: In initial.md, explicitly list pitfalls you've observed: 'Common mistake: handles env vars incorrectly. Correct approach: ...' This directly steers assistant behavior
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: Provide concrete examples (past projects, GitHub repos, official docs) in PRP—examples are more effective for AI coding than abstract documentation
actionable: In PRP examples section, include 3-5 working code samples from similar projects or official tutorials; prioritize examples over documentation links
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: PRP validation gates should include asking AI 'What would it take to get to 10/10 confidence?' to identify missing context and improve PRP before code generation
actionable: After AI generates PRP, ask it 'What would make you 10/10 confident in execution?'; incorporate answers back into PRP; iterate until confidence is high before code generation
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Kira) all support context engineering via slash commands or prompt instructions; PRP is assistant-agnostic
actionable: Adopt PRP framework regardless of AI assistant; translate slash commands to plain prompts if needed; framework is tool-independent
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint

claim: Pydantic AI agents should always authenticate via environment variables; never hardcode credentials; configuration must be modular for dev/prod deployment
actionable: In Pydantic AI agent initial.md, specify env var approach for all credentials (API keys, OAuth tokens); include startup/shutdown methods for initialization
source_title: Build ANY AI Agent with this Context Engineering Blueprint
